steps.title: Find the organizing focus
steps.content:
The passage opens by identifying the Old Manse as a specific house, then immediately states “Now the house is a museum” and spends the rest of the text describing what that museum does—why visitors come and how curators facilitate their connection to Emerson.

The passage's structure and content revolve entirely around describing the museum itself.

steps.title: Match the correct answer
steps.content:
Choice C states the purpose directly: to describe a museum focused on Emerson's life and works.

The passage does precisely that—it identifies the Old Manse as a museum, explains who visits and why, and details curator activities that foster visitor connection.
  • A overshoots into full biography
  • B addresses reader enjoyment broadly
  • D requires a comparison never made
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
